title: OAuth2 credentials failing with 401 in active workflow
symptom: The OAuth2 credential works during manual execution but fails with a 401 error in the active workflow. This indicates an authentication issue.
not_this_if: Error does not occur in manual execution
root_cause: The 401 error can occur due to several reasons, including a changed encryption key that prevents the decryption of stored credentials, clock drift causing tokens to be seen as expired, or missing persistent storage for credentials in containerized environments.
fix_steps: Ensure the N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY is consistent across all runs.
Synchronize your server time using NTP to avoid clock drift.
Verify that the .n8n directory is stored on a persistent volume if using containerized deployment.
agent_instruction: Check and ensure the encryption key is static, server time is synchronized, and credential storage is persistent.
